title: Explain & Review This Code
author: PromptDeck
description: Explains a code snippet and flags bugs, security issues, and improvements.
prompt:
Role: You are a senior software engineer doing a careful review.

Task: For the code below in [LANGUAGE/FRAMEWORK], explain it and improve it. Flag real bugs, edge cases, and security concerns.

Format: (1) 2–3 sentence summary; (2) step-by-step logic in plain language; (3) bugs/edge cases/security issues; (4) concrete improvements with short code examples; (5) quality rating out of 10 with a one-line justification. Code: [PASTE CODE].

title: Write & Explain a SQL Query
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes an optimized SQL query from plain English and explains it with index tips.
prompt:
Role: You are a senior data engineer.

Task: Write a [POSTGRES/MYSQL] query for [DESCRIBE WHAT YOU WANT] against tables/columns [SCHEMA]. Use CTEs for readability and note any assumptions.

Format: (1) the formatted query; (2) a plain-English explanation; (3) performance notes and a helpful index; (4) one alternative approach if it exists.

title: Regex Builder & Explainer
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds a tested regular expression from a description and explains each part.
prompt:
Role: You are a regex expert.

Task: Create a regex for [DESCRIBE THE PATTERN, with should-match and should-not-match examples] in flavor [JS/PYTHON/PCRE]. Keep it as simple as possible while correct.

Format: (1) the regex; (2) a token-by-token breakdown; (3) 3 matching and 3 non-matching test strings; (4) a note on edge cases or backtracking risk.

title: Generate Unit Tests for This Code
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a thorough unit-test suite covering happy paths, edge cases, and errors.
prompt:
Role: You are a test engineer.

Task: Write unit tests for the code below using [LANGUAGE / TEST FRAMEWORK]. Cover happy path, edge cases, invalid inputs, and error handling; mock external dependencies; use behavior-describing test names.

Format: The test file, then a short list of any ambiguous behaviors that need clarification. Code: [PASTE CODE].

title: Commit Message & PR Description Writer
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns a diff into a conventional-commit message and a reviewer-friendly PR description.
prompt:
Role: You are a disciplined senior engineer.

Task: From the change summary or diff below, write a Conventional Commits message and a PR description that's skimmable for a busy reviewer.

Format: (1) commit — type(scope): subject (≤72 chars, imperative) + a short body of what & why; (2) PR description — Summary, What changed, Why, How to test, and a checklist. Changes: [PASTE DIFF OR SUMMARY].

title: Debug This Error
author: PromptDeck
description: Diagnoses an error with ranked likely causes and concrete fixes.
prompt:
Role: You are a senior engineer debugging a production issue.

Task: Diagnose the error below given context [WHAT I WAS DOING], environment [LANGUAGE/FRAMEWORK/VERSION], and code [PASTE CODE].

Format: (1) the 3 most likely root causes, ranked with why; (2) the fastest way to confirm each; (3) a concrete fix for the most likely one; (4) how to prevent this class of bug. Error: [PASTE ERROR/STACK TRACE].

title: Refactor & Clean Up Code
author: PromptDeck
description: Refactors code for readability, performance, and best practices without changing behavior.
prompt:
Role: You are a staff engineer who values clean, maintainable code.

Task: Refactor the code below in [LANGUAGE/FRAMEWORK] to improve readability, structure, and performance while preserving behavior. Follow idiomatic conventions.

Format: (1) the refactored code; (2) a bullet list of what changed and why; (3) any behavior you kept identical to confirm; (4) optional further improvements. Code: [PASTE CODE].

title: REST/JSON API Endpoint Design
author: PromptDeck
description: Designs clean API endpoints with methods, schemas, status codes, and errors.
prompt:
Role: You are an API architect.

Task: Design API endpoints for the feature [FEATURE] with resources [RESOURCES]. Follow REST conventions, sensible status codes, and consistent error shapes.

Format: For each endpoint — method + path; purpose; request params/body schema; success response schema; error responses; auth notes. Then 3 design decisions and any pagination/versioning recommendation.

title: Technical Documentation Writer
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes clear developer docs (README, usage, examples) for code or a library.
prompt:
Role: You are a developer-experience writer.

Task: Write documentation for [PROJECT/MODULE] that does [WHAT], for developers who want to [GOAL]. Base it on the code/details below.

Format: A README with — one-line description; installation; quick-start example; core usage with code snippets; API/options reference table; common pitfalls; and a troubleshooting section. Details: [PASTE CODE/DETAILS].

title: Convert Code Between Languages
author: PromptDeck
description: Translates code from one language/framework to another idiomatically.
prompt:
Role: You are a polyglot senior engineer.

Task: Convert the code below from [SOURCE LANGUAGE] to [TARGET LANGUAGE/FRAMEWORK], writing it the idiomatic way in the target — not a literal line-by-line port. Preserve behavior.

Format: (1) the converted code; (2) notes on idiomatic changes you made; (3) any library equivalents used; (4) anything that behaves differently to watch for. Code: [PASTE CODE].

title: Dockerfile & Container Setup
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes an optimized, secure Dockerfile for your app with an explanation.
prompt:
Role: You are a DevOps engineer.

Task: Write a production-ready Dockerfile for a [STACK] app that [BUILD/RUN DETAILS]. Optimize for small image size, layer caching, and security (non-root, minimal base).

Format: (1) the Dockerfile with brief inline comments; (2) an explanation of key choices; (3) a matching .dockerignore; (4) example build & run commands. Details: [PASTE DETAILS].

title: Git Command Helper
author: PromptDeck
description: Gives the exact git commands (and safe alternatives) for what you want to do.
prompt:
Role: You are a git expert who prevents footguns.

Task: I want to [DESCRIBE GIT GOAL]. My current situation: [CONTEXT/STATE]. Give me the safest way to do it.

Format: (1) the exact commands in order; (2) what each does in plain English; (3) how to undo if it goes wrong; (4) a warning if any step is destructive and a safer alternative.

title: Solve & Explain an Algorithm Problem
author: PromptDeck
description: Solves a coding/algorithm problem with approach, complexity, and clean code.
prompt:
Role: You are a competitive programmer and interview coach.

Task: Solve the problem below in [LANGUAGE]. Explain your reasoning before the code and analyze complexity.

Format: (1) restate the problem & constraints; (2) approach and why; (3) time & space complexity; (4) clean, commented solution; (5) edge cases handled; (6) one optimization if possible. Problem: [PASTE PROBLEM].

title: Add Clear Comments & Docstrings
author: PromptDeck
description: Adds helpful comments and docstrings to code without changing logic.
prompt:
Role: You are an engineer who writes comments that explain 'why', not 'what'.

Task: Add comments and docstrings to the code below in [LANGUAGE], following [DOC STYLE, e.g. JSDoc/Google]. Explain intent and non-obvious decisions; don't state the obvious or change logic.

Format: The fully commented code, then a one-line note on any part that looks confusing and might deserve refactoring. Code: [PASTE CODE].

title: Creative / Design Brief Generator
author: PromptDeck
description: Produces a clear design brief so a designer or AI tool knows exactly what to create.
prompt:
Role: You are a design lead writing briefs.

Task: Write a design brief for [WHAT NEEDS DESIGNING] for brand [BRAND], goal [GOAL], audience [WHO], desired feeling [ADJECTIVES]. Make it clear enough to start without a meeting.

Format: Objective; audience; key message; deliverables & specs; mandatory elements; style/tone references to look for; what to avoid; timeline placeholder; how success is judged.

title: Brand Color Palette & Rationale
author: PromptDeck
description: Suggests a cohesive brand color palette with hex codes, roles, and psychology.
prompt:
Role: You are a brand and UI designer.

Task: Suggest a color palette for [BRAND] (sells [WHAT], wants to feel [ADJECTIVES], audience [WHO]). Keep it cohesive and modern; avoid clashing hues.

Format: Primary, secondary, and 2 accent colors with hex; neutral/background and text colors with hex; the role and 'when to use' per color; a one-line psychology rationale each; a WCAG contrast note for text pairings.

title: UX Microcopy & Empty States
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes clear, on-brand microcopy for buttons, errors, empty states, and tooltips.
prompt:
Role: You are a UX writer.

Task: Write microcopy for [FEATURE/SCREEN] in [PRODUCT], brand voice [VOICE]. Keep it concise, human, action-oriented; no jargon or dead-ends.

Format: Copy for — primary button (2 options); secondary/cancel button; success message; error message (helpful, not blaming); empty state with a nudge (2 options); tooltip.

title: Page Wireframe & Layout Plan
author: PromptDeck
description: Outlines a page's sections and layout in a logical, conversion-aware order.
prompt:
Role: You are a product designer.

Task: Plan a wireframe for a [PAGE TYPE, e.g. SaaS landing page] whose goal is [GOAL] for [AUDIENCE]. Order sections for flow and conversion.

Format: A top-to-bottom section list — for each: purpose, key content, primary element/CTA, and a note on hierarchy. End with mobile considerations and the single most important above-the-fold element.

title: Design System Tokens & Scale
author: PromptDeck
description: Defines foundational design tokens (type, spacing, color, radius) for consistency.
prompt:
Role: You are a design systems lead.

Task: Define foundational design tokens for [PRODUCT/BRAND] with vibe [ADJECTIVES]. Keep scales consistent and accessible.

Format: Token sets with values — color (semantic + palette, hex); typography scale (sizes/weights/line-heights); spacing scale; border-radius; shadows. Present as name/value pairs ready to drop into CSS variables or a tokens file.

title: Accessibility (a11y) Review
author: PromptDeck
description: Reviews a UI description or markup for accessibility issues with fixes.
prompt:
Role: You are an accessibility specialist versed in WCAG 2.2.

Task: Review the UI described or the markup below for accessibility issues (contrast, keyboard nav, focus, labels, semantics, alt text, ARIA misuse) and prioritize fixes.

Format: A table — Issue | WCAG criterion | Severity | Fix. Then the top 3 to fix first. Input: [PASTE UI DESCRIPTION OR MARKUP].

title: Design Critique & Improvements
author: PromptDeck
description: Gives structured, constructive critique of a design with concrete improvements.
prompt:
Role: You are a seasoned design critic who is kind but honest.

Task: Critique the design described below (goal: [GOAL], audience: [WHO]) across hierarchy, spacing, typography, color, and clarity of the primary action.

Format: What's working; the top 5 issues ranked by impact, each with a specific fix; and the single highest-leverage change to make first. Design: [DESCRIBE OR PASTE DETAILS].

title: Font Pairing Recommendations
author: PromptDeck
description: Recommends harmonious font pairings with usage and fallbacks.
prompt:
Role: You are a typographer.

Task: Recommend 3 font pairings for [BRAND/PROJECT] that should feel [ADJECTIVES], for use on [WEB/PRINT/APP]. Prefer accessible, widely available (e.g. Google Fonts) options.

Format: For each pairing — heading font + body font, why they work together, suggested weights/sizes, a web-safe fallback stack, and where it fits best. Recommend the strongest.

title: Landing Hero Concepts (Copy + Visual)
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates hero-section concepts pairing headline copy with a visual direction.
prompt:
Role: You are a creative director for web.

Task: Create 3 hero-section concepts for [PRODUCT] targeting [AUDIENCE] with goal [GOAL]. Each pairs copy with a visual direction.

Format: For each concept — headline, subheadline, CTA text, and a described visual/layout direction (imagery, style, mood). Note which converts best and why.

title: Midjourney Prompt Generator
author: PromptDeck
description: Outputs four richly detailed, ready-to-paste Midjourney prompts with parameters.
prompt:
Role: You are a Midjourney prompt expert.

Task: For the concept [CONCEPT], write 4 distinct, ready-to-paste prompts, each specifying subject and action, environment, art style/medium, lighting, color palette, mood, camera/composition, and detail level. Vary the style across the four (photoreal, cinematic, illustrated, 3D).

Format: 4 numbered prompts, each ending with sensible parameters (e.g. --ar 16:9 --style raw --v 6). Output only the prompts.

title: Improve My Prompt (Meta-Prompt)
author: PromptDeck
description: Rewrites a rough prompt into a clear, structured, high-performing one with variables.
prompt:
Role: You are a prompt engineer.

Task: Improve the prompt below so it produces better, more reliable results. Point out what's vague, then rewrite it with a clear role, task, context, constraints, output format, and [FILL-IN VARIABLES].

Format: (1) what's weak; (2) the rewritten, copy-paste-ready prompt; (3) a short 'why this is better'; (4) one optional advanced version. Prompt: [PASTE PROMPT].

title: Custom GPT / Assistant System Prompt
author: PromptDeck
description: Designs a robust system prompt for a custom GPT, chatbot, or AI assistant.
prompt:
Role: You are an AI systems designer.

Task: Write a system prompt for an assistant that does [PURPOSE] for users [WHO], with personality [TONE]. It must always [KEY BEHAVIORS] and never [GUARDRAILS].

Format: A complete system prompt covering role & goal, tone, step-by-step behavior, output format, handling of unknowns/out-of-scope, and safety guardrails. Then 3 test messages to verify it behaves correctly.

title: AI Image Prompt (DALL·E / SD)
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes detailed image-generation prompts for DALL·E or Stable Diffusion with a negative prompt.
prompt:
Role: You are an AI image prompt specialist.

Task: Write image prompts for [CONCEPT] for tool [DALL·E/STABLE DIFFUSION], use case [LOGO/HERO/ILLUSTRATION]. Each specifies subject, composition, style/medium, lighting, color, mood, and detail level.

Format: 3 ready-to-paste variations. For Stable Diffusion also include a negative prompt and suggested settings.

title: Few-Shot Example Builder
author: PromptDeck
description: Creates high-quality few-shot examples to make an LLM output consistent.
prompt:
Role: You are a prompt engineer specializing in in-context learning.

Task: Build few-shot examples for the task [TASK], where inputs look like [INPUT FORMAT] and outputs should look like [DESIRED OUTPUT]. Make examples diverse and cover edge cases.

Format: A short instruction line, then 4–6 input→output example pairs formatted consistently, then a note on how to add the user's real input. Keep formatting machine-friendly.

title: AI Agent Instructions & Tools Spec
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes clear instructions and a tool-use policy for an autonomous AI agent.
prompt:
Role: You are an AI agent architect.

Task: Write instructions for an agent whose job is [GOAL], with access to tools [TOOLS]. Define how it plans, when to use each tool, and when to ask the user or stop.

Format: Role & objective; operating loop (plan→act→observe→reflect); tool-use rules per tool; guardrails & stop conditions; output format for the final answer; 2 example task walk-throughs.

title: Reverse-Engineer a Prompt from a Description
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns a described image/style into a reusable generation prompt.
prompt:
Role: You are an expert at deconstructing visual style into prompts.

Task: From the image or style I describe [DESCRIBE THE LOOK], write a reusable generation prompt that would recreate it, isolating the style elements from the subject so I can swap subjects.

Format: (1) a full prompt; (2) a 'style-only' snippet I can append to any subject; (3) key parameters; (4) 2 variations for different subjects.

title: Logo Concept & Generation Prompt
author: PromptDeck
description: Develops logo concepts and matching AI image prompts for a brand.
prompt:
Role: You are a logo designer who also writes AI image prompts.

Task: Develop logo directions for [BRAND] (industry [INDUSTRY], feeling [ADJECTIVES], audience [WHO]). Then write prompts to generate them.

Format: 3 concepts — each with the idea, symbolism, style (wordmark/lettermark/icon), and color direction; plus a ready-to-paste image prompt. Note that AI logos need vector cleanup and to verify originality.
